arguments.file_text:
# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Problem

5 MSTest projects currently on VSTest (MSTest 3.8.0, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0) need to
migrate to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. CI uses Azure DevOps with VSTest@3, TRX reporting, and
code coverage. Target SDK is .NET 8.

## Approach

Incremental migration — one project at a time — using MSTest's built-in MTP support. VSTest@3
task is replaced with `dotnet test` (DotNetCoreCLI@2). TRX and coverage are preserved via MTP
extension packages.

---

## Phase 1: Prepare — Central Package Management & Directory.Build.props

### 1.1 Update MSTest packages to ≥ 3.8.2 (latest 3.x patch)

MSTest 3.8.x has mature MTP support. Bump to latest patch for bug fixes:

```xml
<!-- In Directory.Packages.props or each .csproj -->
<PackageVersion Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.2" />
<PackageVersion Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.2" />
<PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
```

### 1.2 Verify baseline — all 5 projects pass on VSTest before any changes

```bash
dotnet test MySolution.sln --logger trx
```

---

## Phase 2: Convert Projects to MTP (one at a time)

For **each** test project, apply these changes:

### 2.1 Edit the .csproj

**Remove** these package references:

```xml
<!-- DELETE these two -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
```

**Add/modify** these properties and packages:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <!-- Keeps backward compat so `dotnet test` still works -->
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Keep this -->
  <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.2" />
  <!-- TRX report generation (replaces VSTest TRX logger) -->
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
  <!-- Code coverage via Microsoft's built-in collector -->
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
</ItemGroup>
```

> **Key changes explained:**
> - `MSTest` is the unified meta-package (replaces TestFramework + TestAdapter + NET.Test.Sdk).
> - `EnableMSTestRunner` + `OutputType Exe` makes each test project a self-contained executable using MTP.
> - `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` lets `dotnet test` delegate to MTP under the hood.
> - `TrxReport` extension produces `.trx` files identical in schema to VSTest's.
> - `CodeCoverage` extension replaces the VSTest data collector.

### 2.2 (Optional) Consolidate in Directory.Build.props

If all 5 projects share the same settings:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props at solution root -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### 2.3 Remove any .runsettings references to VSTest adapters

If your `.runsettings` file references `MSTest.TestAdapter` paths or VSTest-specific data
collectors, remove or update them. MTP ignores most VSTest `.runsettings` sections. Code
coverage is now configured via `--coverage` CLI flag or a `.testconfig.json` file.

### 2.4 Validate locally

```bash
# Run via dotnet test (backward-compat mode)
dotnet test MyProject.Tests.csproj --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults

# Run as native MTP executable (the new way)
dotnet run --project MyProject.Tests.csproj -- --treenode-filter "/**" --report-trx
```

Both should produce TRX files. Confirm test count matches the old run.

---

## Phase 3: Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

### 3.1 Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2

**Before (VSTest@3):**

```yaml
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**\*Tests.dll'
    resultsFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)\TestResults'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true
```

**After (DotNetCoreCLI@2 + dotnet test):**

```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger "trx;LogFilePrefix=TestResults"
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
      --collect "Code Coverage"
    publishTestResults: true
```

> `publishTestResults: true` auto-publishes TRX files to the Azure DevOps Test tab.

### 3.2 If using MTP-native flags instead of `dotnet test`

You can also invoke the test executables directly for faster runs (no VSTest translation layer):

```yaml
- script: |
    dotnet build MySolution.sln -c Release
  displayName: 'Build'

- script: |
    for testExe in $(find . -path '*/bin/Release/net8.0/*Tests' -type f); do
      $testExe --report-trx --report-trx-filename $(basename $testExe).trx --coverage --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
    done
  displayName: 'Run Tests (MTP native)'

- task: PublishTestResults@2
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.cobertura.xml'
```

### 3.3 Code Coverage Output Format

MTP's `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage` outputs `.cobertura.xml` by default.
If your pipeline used `.coverage` (binary) format before, this is actually an upgrade —
Cobertura XML works natively with `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2` without needing a
conversion step.

---

## Phase 4: Clean Up & Validate

### 4.1 Remove leftover VSTest artifacts

- Delete any `*.runsettings` files that are now unused.
- Remove `MSTest.TestAdapter` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` from `Directory.Packages.props`
  if using central package management.
- Remove any `<VSTestLogger>`, `<VSTestResultsDirectory>`, or `<RunSettingsFilePath>` MSBuild
  properties that are no longer needed.

### 4.2 Verify in CI

- Confirm the Azure DevOps **Tests** tab shows all tests from all 5 projects.
- Confirm the **Code Coverage** tab shows combined coverage.
- Confirm TRX files are being produced and attached to the build.

### 4.3 (Optional) Enable MTP-specific features

Now that you're on MTP, you can leverage features that VSTest didn't have:

- **`--retry-failed-tests N`** — automatic retry of flaky tests.
- **`--maximum-failed-tests N`** — fail-fast after N failures.
- **Parallel per-project** — MTP executables can run in true parallel (separate processes).
- **Hot reload testing** — `dotnet watch` works with MTP test executables.

---

## Todos

1. Verify baseline — all 5 projects green on VSTest
2. Update MSTest packages to 3.8.2
3. Convert project 1 to MTP (csproj changes)
4. Convert project 2 to MTP
5. Convert project 3 to MTP
6. Convert project 4 to MTP
7. Convert project 5 to MTP
8. Consolidate shared settings into Directory.Build.props
9. Update Azure DevOps pipeline (replace VSTest@3)
10. Validate CI — tests tab, coverage tab, TRX output
11. Clean up leftover VSTest artifacts
12. Document new test runner for the team

## Risks & Notes

- **No test source changes needed.** `[TestClass]`, `[TestMethod]`, `[DataRow]`, etc. all work
  identically on MTP. This is a runner/infrastructure change only.
- **Rollback:** Simply revert the `.csproj` changes and pipeline YAML. MSTest 3.8.x works on
  both VSTest and MTP.
- **`.runsettings` partial support:** MTP supports a subset of `.runsettings` (timeout, parallelism).
  If you rely on VSTest-specific settings (HostProcessPlatform, etc.), check
  [MTP .runsettings docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-runsettings).
- **IDE support:** Visual Studio 2022 17.10+ and VS Code with C# Dev Kit both support MTP.
  Rider support is available in 2024.2+.

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Problem

5 MSTest projects currently on VSTest (MSTest 3.8.0, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0) need to
migrate to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. CI uses Azure DevOps with VSTest@3, TRX reporting, and
code coverage. Target SDK is .NET 8.

## Approach

Incremental migration — one project at a time — using MSTest's built-in MTP support. VSTest@3
task is replaced with `dotnet test` (DotNetCoreCLI@2). TRX and coverage are preserved via MTP
extension packages.

---

## Phase 1: Prepare — Central Package Management & Directory.Build.props

### 1.1 Update MSTest packages to ≥ 3.8.2 (latest 3.x patch)

MSTest 3.8.x has mature MTP support. Bump to latest patch for bug fixes:

```xml
<!-- In Directory.Packages.props or each .csproj -->
<PackageVersion Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.2" />
<PackageVersion Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.2" />
<PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
```

### 1.2 Verify baseline — all 5 projects pass on VSTest before any changes

```bash
dotnet test MySolution.sln --logger trx
```

---

## Phase 2: Convert Projects to MTP (one at a time)

For **each** test project, apply these changes:

### 2.1 Edit the .csproj

**Remove** these package references:

```xml
<!-- DELETE these two -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
```

**Add/modify** these properties and packages:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <!-- Keeps backward compat so `dotnet test` still works -->
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Keep this -->
  <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.2" />
  <!-- TRX report generation (replaces VSTest TRX logger) -->
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
  <!-- Code coverage via Microsoft's built-in collector -->
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
</ItemGroup>
```

> **Key changes explained:**
> - `MSTest` is the unified meta-package (replaces TestFramework + TestAdapter + NET.Test.Sdk).
> - `EnableMSTestRunner` + `OutputType Exe` makes each test project a self-contained executable using MTP.
> - `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` lets `dotnet test` delegate to MTP under the hood.
> - `TrxReport` extension produces `.trx` files identical in schema to VSTest's.
> - `CodeCoverage` extension replaces the VSTest data collector.

### 2.2 (Optional) Consolidate in Directory.Build.props

If all 5 projects share the same settings:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props at solution root -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### 2.3 Remove any .runsettings references to VSTest adapters

If your `.runsettings` file references `MSTest.TestAdapter` paths or VSTest-specific data
collectors, remove or update them. MTP ignores most VSTest `.runsettings` sections. Code
coverage is now configured via `--coverage` CLI flag or a `.testconfig.json` file.

### 2.4 Validate locally

```bash
# Run via dotnet test (backward-compat mode)
dotnet test MyProject.Tests.csproj --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults

# Run as native MTP executable (the new way)
dotnet run --project MyProject.Tests.csproj -- --treenode-filter "/**" --report-trx
```

Both should produce TRX files. Confirm test count matches the old run.

---

## Phase 3: Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

### 3.1 Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2

**Before (VSTest@3):**

```yaml
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**\*Tests.dll'
    resultsFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)\TestResults'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true
```

**After (DotNetCoreCLI@2 + dotnet test):**

```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger "trx;LogFilePrefix=TestResults"
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
      --collect "Code Coverage"
    publishTestResults: true
```

> `publishTestResults: true` auto-publishes TRX files to the Azure DevOps Test tab.

### 3.2 If using MTP-native flags instead of `dotnet test`

You can also invoke the test executables directly for faster runs (no VSTest translation layer):

```yaml
- script: |
    dotnet build MySolution.sln -c Release
  displayName: 'Build'

- script: |
    for testExe in $(find . -path '*/bin/Release/net8.0/*Tests' -type f); do
      $testExe --report-trx --report-trx-filename $(basename $testExe).trx --coverage --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
    done
  displayName: 'Run Tests (MTP native)'

- task: PublishTestResults@2
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.cobertura.xml'
```

### 3.3 Code Coverage Output Format

MTP's `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage` outputs `.cobertura.xml` by default.
If your pipeline used `.coverage` (binary) format before, this is actually an upgrade —
Cobertura XML works natively with `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2` without needing a
conversion step.

---

## Phase 4: Clean Up & Validate

### 4.1 Remove leftover VSTest artifacts

- Delete any `*.runsettings` files that are now unused.
- Remove `MSTest.TestAdapter` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` from `Directory.Packages.props`
  if using central package management.
- Remove any `<VSTestLogger>`, `<VSTestResultsDirectory>`, or `<RunSettingsFilePath>` MSBuild
  properties that are no longer needed.

### 4.2 Verify in CI

- Confirm the Azure DevOps **Tests** tab shows all tests from all 5 projects.
- Confirm the **Code Coverage** tab shows combined coverage.
- Confirm TRX files are being produced and attached to the build.

### 4.3 (Optional) Enable MTP-specific features

Now that you're on MTP, you can leverage features that VSTest didn't have:

- **`--retry-failed-tests N`** — automatic retry of flaky tests.
- **`--maximum-failed-tests N`** — fail-fast after N failures.
- **Parallel per-project** — MTP executables can run in true parallel (separate processes).
- **Hot reload testing** — `dotnet watch` works with MTP test executables.

---

## Todos

1. Verify baseline — all 5 projects green on VSTest
2. Update MSTest packages to 3.8.2
3. Convert project 1 to MTP (csproj changes)
4. Convert project 2 to MTP
5. Convert project 3 to MTP
6. Convert project 4 to MTP
7. Convert project 5 to MTP
8. Consolidate shared settings into Directory.Build.props
9. Update Azure DevOps pipeline (replace VSTest@3)
10. Validate CI — tests tab, coverage tab, TRX output
11. Clean up leftover VSTest artifacts
12. Document new test runner for the team

## Risks & Notes

- **No test source changes needed.** `[TestClass]`, `[TestMethod]`, `[DataRow]`, etc. all work
  identically on MTP. This is a runner/infrastructure change only.
- **Rollback:** Simply revert the `.csproj` changes and pipeline YAML. MSTest 3.8.x works on
  both VSTest and MTP.
- **`.runsettings` partial support:** MTP supports a subset of `.runsettings` (timeout, parallelism).
  If you rely on VSTest-specific settings (HostProcessPlatform, etc.), check
  [MTP .runsettings docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-runsettings).
- **IDE support:** Visual Studio 2022 17.10+ and VS Code with C# Dev Kit both support MTP.
  Rider support is available in 2024.2+.


## Phase 2: Convert Projects to MTP (one at a time)

For **each** test project, apply these changes:

### 2.1 Edit the .csproj

**Remove** these package references:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
```

**Add/modify** these properties and packages:

```xml

  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <!-- Keeps backward compat so `dotnet test` still works -->
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Keep this -->
  <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.2" />
  <!-- TRX report generation (replaces VSTest TRX logger) -->
```

4. Convert project 2 to MTP
5. Convert project 3 to MTP

7. Convert project 5 to MTP
8. Consolidate shared settings into Directory.Build.props
9. Update Azure DevOps pipeline (replace VSTest@3)

## Summary: 4 Phases

**Phase 1 — Prepare:** Bump MSTest to 3.8.2 (latest patch), verify all 5 projects pass on VSTest as a baseline.

- **Add** `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` (replaces VSTest TRX logger) and `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage`
- Set `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true` so `dotnet test` still works

**Phase 3 — Update Azure DevOps pipeline:**
- Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2` (or invoke MTP executables directly)
- TRX + Cobertura coverage publish natively — no conversion step needed

**Phase 4 — Clean up:** Remove leftover `.runsettings`, old package refs, validate CI tabs.

### Key points
- **Zero test code changes** — all MSTest attributes work identically
- **Easy rollback** — revert `.csproj` + YAML; MSTest 3.8.x supports both runners
- **New capabilities unlocked:** `--retry-failed-tests`, `--maximum-failed-tests`, true per-project parallelism
